title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Contract Triage
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible file metadata and object reconciliation state machine with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in file metadata and object reconciliation state machine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
Persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
Choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Orphan Repair
steps.instruction:
Order the Orphan Repair S3, CloudFront, and Go storage-delivery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Orphan Repair.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Orphan Repair assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Orphan Repair, keep Orphan Repair connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 7040 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Orphan Repair has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Orphan Repair, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Orphan Repair precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Orphan Repair, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 7040 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Orphan Repair, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Deleting every unreferenced key found in one list pass is safe garbage collection.

options.text: Declare S3, CloudFront, and Go stakeholder outcome, file and object identity, owner, state, and byte, time, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets for the debug Orphan Repair case
Predict S3, CloudFront, and Go authorization, custody, transfer, integrity, cache, failure, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the debug Orphan Repair case
Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the debug Orphan Repair case
Inject one changed S3, CloudFront, and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup and recovery, and state controlled AWS transfer limits for the debug Orphan Repair case

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug System Of Record Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 8659 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.

Inspect the System Of Record Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

stimulus.title: System Of Record Boundary case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 8659 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One system can atomically own database rows and S3 objects in a normal transaction.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation. Artifact under review: file metadata and object reconciliation state machine.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies System Of Record Boundary, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The System Of Record Boundary record says file metadata and object reconciliation state machine looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for System Of Record Boundary.

For inspect the failure record, run one convenient System Of Record Boundary happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy System Of Record Boundary for file metadata and object reconciliation state machine.
